title: Discount Calculator (with optional sales tax)
shortDescription: Calculate the final price after a percentage discount, plus optional sales tax. See how much you saved at a glance.
description: Enter the original price and the discount percentage to see the final price and how much you saved. Add a sales tax rate if your jurisdiction adds tax after the discount.
metaDescription: Free discount calculator. Compute final price after a percentage discount, with optional sales tax. See savings, after-discount price, and tax breakdown instantly.
empty: Enter an original price and discount to see the result.

sections.heading: How discount math works
paragraphs: Discounted price = original × (1 − discount/100). A 20% discount on a $100 item leaves $80. Sales tax is applied to the discounted amount, not the original — so $80 × (1 + 8% tax/100) = $86.40 final.
The 'you save' amount is the discount in dollars: original × discount/100. For our $100 example at 20% off, you save $20. This is what shoppers naturally want to see — not just the percent.

sections.heading: Stacking discounts
paragraphs: Two discounts don't add. A '20% off' coupon on top of a '20% off sale' is 36% off total, not 40% — because the second discount applies to the already-reduced price (0.8 × 0.8 = 0.64 = 36% off the original).

To stack, run the calculator twice: first with the bigger discount, then plug the result back in as the new 'original' with the second discount. Or, if you have multiple coupons, multiply (1 − d1/100) × (1 − d2/100) × ... and subtract from 1 to get the total effective discount.

sections.heading: Tax timing varies by country
paragraphs: United States: sales tax is added at the register, after any discounts. Use this calculator's tax field directly. Each state and city has its own rate (NYC 8.875%, LA 9.5%, no tax in Oregon).

EU/UK: VAT is typically already in the displayed price, so don't double-count. Set tax to 0 if the price you entered is VAT-inclusive. Japan's consumption tax is also usually included in displayed prices (総額表示).
Sometimes shipping is taxable, sometimes not. This calculator assumes the price you enter is the taxable amount. For complete cart math, total your taxable items separately.

howTo.name: Enter the original price
howTo.text: Use the listed price before any discount.

howTo.name: Optional: enter tax %
howTo.text: Skip if tax is already included or doesn't apply.

howTo.name: Read the breakdown
howTo.text: Final price, savings, after-discount, and tax all appear together.

faq.q: Is the discount applied before or after tax?
faq.a: Almost always before. The discount reduces the taxable amount; tax is then added to the discounted price.

faq.q: How do I stack multiple coupons?
faq.a: Apply them sequentially: original × (1 − d1/100) × (1 − d2/100). Use the calculator twice or compute manually.
Final = original × (1 − discount/100). Savings = original × discount/100.
Roughly — BOGO half off is effectively a 25% discount on a two-item bundle. For exact BOGO math, divide your bundle price into per-item amounts first.

faq.q: Why did I see a 'comparison' price that wasn't real?
faq.a: Some retailers inflate the 'original' price to make discounts look bigger. Compare to other stores or historical prices to know the real discount.

faq.q: Are stacking 'mystery' discount codes worth it?
faq.a: Run the math both ways. Often a single big code beats two small ones because of the multiplicative effect.

faq.q: How do I find the discount % from two prices?
faq.a: Discount % = (original − sale) / original × 100. Or use our percentage calculator's 'percent change' mode.
No. Calculation runs in your browser.
